steps.title: Align the hypothetical timing
steps.content: The main clause uses a hypothetical past form: the sugars could have reacted. The sugars first needed to reach Earth and survive impact before that reaction could happen.

steps.title: Use past perfect tense
steps.content: A uses present tense, C uses future tense, and D uses present progressive tense. The matching verb is past perfect, which correctly marks an earlier past action before the later hypothetical reaction. The answer is B.
